title: Ensure that the --hostname-override argument is not set

input_row.title: Ensure that the --hostname-override argument is not set
input_row.rationale: Overriding hostnames could potentially break TLS setup between the kubelet and the apiserver. Additionally, with overridden hostnames, it becomes increasingly difficult to associate logs with a particular node and process them for security analytics. Hence, you should setup your kubelet nodes with resolvable FQDNs and avoid overriding the hostnames with IPs. Impact: Some cloud providers may require this flag to ensure that hostname matches names issued by the cloud provider. In these environments, this recommendation should not apply.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on each node: ps -ef | grep kubelet Verify that --hostname-override argument does not exist. Note This setting is not configurable via the Kubelet config file.
input_row.remediation: Edit the kubelet service file /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/10-kubeadm.conf on each worker node and remove the --hostname-override argument from the KUBELET_SYSTEM_PODS_ARGS variable. Based on your system, restart the kubelet service. For example: systemctl daemon-reload systemctl restart kubelet.service Default Value: By default, --hostname-override argument is not set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kubelet/ 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/22063
input_row.profile_applicability: •  Level 1 - Worker Node •  Level 2 - Worker Node
input_row.impact: Some cloud providers may require this flag to ensure that hostname matches names issued by the cloud provider. In these environments, this recommendation should not apply.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control requires verification that the kubelet process on each node is NOT started with the --hostname-override argument.
- The CIS audit procedure specifies running ps -ef | grep kubelet on each node and visually inspecting the process arguments.
- This flag is NOT configurable via the Kubelet config file and is only set via the process/systemd unit, which is not exposed via the Kubernetes API or kubectl.
- Kubernetes APIs do not expose the process arguments or systemd unit files of the kubelet running on nodes.
- This check requires host-level access (SSH or equivalent) to examine the process command line or systemd configuration.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into each Kubernetes worker node.
2. Run the following command to list the kubelet process and its arguments:
   ```
   ps -ef | grep kubelet
   ```
3. Review the output and verify that the kubelet process is NOT started with the --hostname-override argument.
4. Alternatively, inspect the kubelet systemd service file (commonly at /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/10-kubeadm.conf or similar) to ensure --hostname-override is not present in the KUBELET_SYSTEM_PODS_ARGS or other argument variables.
5. Confirm compliance if --hostname-override is absent from both the running process and the service configuration.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.7.1 PDF
